channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Durham Police Offer $15,000 Reward in Oshawa Homicide Case as Family Seeks Answers
item.description: Durham Regional Police have announced a $15,000 reward for information that could lead to the arrest and conviction of those responsible for a recent homicide in Oshawa. The investigation follows the ...

item.title: Understanding Kenya’s Electricity Shortages and Steps to Improve Reliability
item.description: Since late 2024, Kenya has experienced frequent electricity supply interruptions due to insufficient production capacity and growing demand. President William Ruto acknowledged that daily load-sheddin...

item.title: Delta Governor Plays 'This or That' Game, Picks Davido Over Wizkid
item.description: On March 9, 2026, Delta State Governor Sheriff Oborevwori participate for one viral 'This or That' game wey the official Delta State X account post. Di game cover plenty things like preferred cars, co...

item.title: Magnitude 4.4 Earthquake Near Boorowa Shakes Canberra and South-East NSW
item.description: On the evening of 11 March 2026, a magnitude 4.4 earthquake struck near Boorowa in New South Wales at 7:09pm, with tremors felt across Canberra and much of south-east NSW. Initially recorded as magnit...

item.title: Kenyan MPs Propose Mandatory Guidance and Counselling in All Schools
item.description: Kenyan MPs are pushing hard to make guidance and counselling a must in all schools across the country. They say this will help learners deal with rising cases of indiscipline, drug abuse, mental healt...

item.title: Exhibition at London’s Opera Gallery Explores Surrealism and Magical Realism in Contemporary Art
item.description: Dreaming in Colour, currently showing at the Opera Gallery in London, presents a diverse range of contemporary works alongside historic pieces by artists such as Picasso, Chagall, and Ron Arad. The ex...

item.title: Astronomical Forecast: Eid ul Fitr 2026 in Pakistan Expected on March 21
item.description: According to Suparco, Pakistan’s Eid ul Fitr in 2026 is expected to fall on Saturday, March 21, following a complete 30-day month of Ramadan. The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commissio...

item.title: Amit Shah defends Speaker Om Birla as Opposition's no-confidence motion fails in Lok Sabha
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah strongly criticised the Opposition in the Lok Sabha on Wednesday for moving a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla. Shah argued that questioning the Speaker's ro...

item.title: Kenyan Artist Reimagines Kirinyaga in 2125 Using Virtual Reality
item.description: Kenyan digital artist San’aa Njeeri, formerly Jackie Njeri, is pushing the boundaries of African creativity with her latest project, Area Nyaga. Presented at Baraza Media Lab, the project envisions Ki...

item.title: The Rosenstrasse Protest: German Wives Defy Nazi Regime to Save Their Husbands
item.description: On February 27, 1943, a small group of non-Jewish women gathered on Rosenstrasse in central Berlin to demand the release of their Jewish husbands, who had been arrested in a mass roundup of over 9,000...

item.title: Maltina Launches Nationwide School Tour to Promote Nutrition for Children
item.description: Maltina don celebrate 50 years for Nigeria and dem dey use the opportunity run nationwide school tour wey dem call the Maltina Nourishment Tour. The tour dey aimed at helping pikin dem understand how ...

item.title: Analysis Shows Certain Areas Where Buying a Unit Can Be Comparable or Cheaper Than Renting
item.description: Recent research from Cotality indicates that, in select regions of Australia, it can be almost as economical—or in some cases cheaper—to pay a mortgage than rent, particularly for units rather than st...

item.title: Healthy Nigerian Foods to Help You Stay Cool During Hot Weather
item.description: During Naija’s hot season wey dey run from February reach May, heat fit rise pass 40°C for many areas, making plenty people struggle to stay comfortable. This article yarn say wetin you chop fit help ...

item.title: House of Representatives encounters challenges in approving travel tax abolition
item.description: The proposal to abolish the travel tax in the Philippines has encountered significant challenges during its second reading in the House of Representatives. Lawmakers are debating the bill amid discuss...

item.title: Remembering Biodun Jeyifo: Scholar, Activist and Revolutionary
item.description: Dis story na tribute to Biodun Jeyifo (BJ), one scholar wey no just sabi book but also sabi fight for justice. BJ waka through many universities like Ibadan, Ife, Oberlin, Cornell, Harvard, Berlin and...

item.title: Evidence shows Shibiri had repeated contact with Matlala prior to arrest
item.description: New evidence presented at the Madlanga Commission challenges Maj-Gen Richard Shibiri’s claim that he had minimal communication with Vusi 'Cat' Matlala. Despite Shibiri’s statement that he does not rec...

item.title: Astronomical Predictions Suggest Eidul Fitr on March 21
item.description: The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission, Suparco, has forecast that Eidul Fitr 1447 AH is likely to be observed on Saturday, March 21, 2026. This prediction comes as the Shawwal mo...

item.title: IRCTC shifts station kitchens to microwaves and induction; PM Modi offers support to Indians stranded in Gulf
item.description: Indian Railways, via the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC), has instructed station food outlets, including food plazas, refreshment rooms, and Jan Aahar canteens, to switch to al...

item.title: UK player wins £181 million in EuroMillions jackpot
item.description: A UK lottery player has won an astounding £181,073,415.70 in Tuesday's EuroMillions draw, making it the third-largest National Lottery prize in UK history. The lucky winner correctly selected all five...

item.title: Shibiri’s calls with Matlala contradict his statements to parliamentary committee
item.description: Evidence presented to the parliamentary ad hoc committee investigating corruption in the criminal justice system shows that Maj-Gen Richard Shibiri, suspended head of the SAPS organised crime division...
